verdict_reasoning: BTK is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase with a pleckstrin homology domain that binds phosphatidylinositol phosphates on the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane during BCR signaling. While it transiently associates with the PM, this association is cytoplasmic-face anchoring via inner-leaflet lipid recognition, not outer-leaflet accessibility. BTK lacks transmembrane domains, GPI anchors, or secretory signals and is not deployed as a stable non-covalent partner of a TM surface protein.
